commands.repair_reason: No automated repair is needed; maintainers should choose the canonical UX between this PR and the open sibling, then let CI and normal review gate the selected branch.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain issue found; the diff emits a fixed literal, keeps SSRF/fetch behavior unchanged, and tests that URL/token-bearing data is not used as channel progress text. (sha=94cdaad417d9c57697548f735765244910820ac3)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a delayed single-shot `web_fetch` progress emit, maps allow-listed non-exec partial results to item `progressText`, omits update `meta` so channel drafts render the progress text, and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +106, Tests +552. Total +658 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main has no `web_fetch` `onUpdate` forwarding or item `progressText`, and the PR body gives a concrete slow-URL proof path through the shared formatter.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is needed; maintainers should choose the canonical UX between this PR and the open sibling, then let CI and normal review gate the selected branch.

target.title: fix(web-fetch): surface progress during long fetches
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

`web_fetch` produces a fully silent gap between agent invocation and tool result during slow TTFB or slow body streams. Channel UIs render that gap as the URL meta line ("from \<url\>") for the entire fetch duration — a visible source of friction for slow-TTFB and slow-body-stream fetches.

Why does this matter now?

`progressText` is already the canonical channel-visible progress field, plumbed through `src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.ts:buildChannelProgressDraftLine`. Adding a single allow-listed non-exec tool — `web_fetch` — reuses the same field on the existing `kind:"tool"` item with no channel-adapter changes.

What changed since round 1 of ClawSweeper review?

ClawSweeper round 1 flagged a real precedence bug: the channel draft formatter is `input.meta ?? input.summary ?? input.progressText`, so the URL-bearing web_fetch `meta` always won over the new `progressText`. **Commit `94cdaad4` fixes this** by omitting `meta` on the runtime update event whenever non-exec `progressText` is populated, so the formatter falls through to `progressText`. A new formatter-integration test and a Tier-B real-behavior proof confirm the channel-visible text is now `"Fetching web page…"` rather than the URL meta. **Implementation scope remains the same 2 production files; only one targeted precedence fix was added.**

What is the intended outcome?

Channel users see a single generic `"Fetching web page…"` line after ~1 s when the network work has not returned. Fast fetches stay silent. Fetch result, error type, cache, timeout, abort, retry, redirects, and SSRF guard are unchanged.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Per-tool progress for other built-in tools (browser, image_generate, video_generate, mcp).
- MCP `notifications/progress` plumbing.
- Generalized non-exec rate-limit redesign or progress-infrastructure refactor.
- Channel renderer or `channel-streaming.ts` changes: untouched.
- `extensions/*` channel adapters: untouched.
- Adding non-`web_fetch` tools to the runtime allow-list.

What should reviewers focus on?

- `src/agents/tools/web-fetch.ts`: outer `try/finally` around the existing network/extract body releases the timer exactly once across every exit path. `emitWebFetchProgressSafely` swallows a throwing `onUpdate`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts`: `NON_EXEC_PROGRESS_SAFE_TOOLS = Set("web_fetch")` is the contract boundary. Adding a tool requires auditing its `onUpdate` callsite. **Precedence fix (`94cdaad4`)**: when `nonExecProgressText` is populated, omit `meta` on the update event so the channel draft formatter falls through to `progressText`. The start event still carries `meta` for consumers that snapshot it at item start.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

No upstream issue. Driven by a candidate selection report from masato-local UX-perf evaluation.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

None directly.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No specific maintainer request. Round 1 ClawSweeper review (`@clawsweeper` automated) identified the precedence gap; this PR body update reflects the fix.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `web_fetch` channel-visible dead air. Pre-patch, channel UIs showed `"📄 Web Fetch: from <url>"` for the full silent gap. Post-patch, after ~1 s of silence, the channel draft transitions to `"📄 Web Fetch: Fetching web page…"` and stays there until the fetch completes.
- Real environment tested: Node 22.22.2 / Linux 6.8.0-111-generic / live network. Slow path = `https://httpbin.org/delay/3` (3 s server-side hold; loopback would require modifying the SSRF guard which is intentionally out of scope). Fast path = `https://example.com/` (~400 ms). Tier-B integration: real `createWebFetchTool().execute()` → real `handleToolExecutionUpdate` → real `buildChannelProgressDraftLine` formatter, exercising the production chain from tool callback to channel-visible draft text.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```
  cd worktrees/openclaw-c2-web-fetch-tool-progress

  # Tier-B integration: tool → runtime → channel formatter
  PROOF_HEAD=$(git rev-parse HEAD) \
    node --import tsx \
    .local/c2-tool-progress-web-fetch/clawsweeper-remediation-20260526T171156Z/run-real-proof.mjs

  # Targeted unit + regression at current HEAD
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/web-fetch.progress.test.ts --run
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts --run
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts --run
  ```

- Evidence after fix (terminal transcript, HEAD = `94cdaad417d9c57697548f735765244910820ac3`):

  ```
  === PR #86965 Tier-B real behavior proof ===
  HEAD: 94cdaad417d9c57697548f735765244910820ac3
  Driver: real createWebFetchTool().execute → real handleToolExecutionUpdate → real buildChannelProgressDraftLine

  --- slow path: https://httpbin.org/delay/3 ---
  total=4018ms
  start.meta="from https://httpbin.org/delay/3"
  update_item_events=1
    [0] kind=tool progressText="Fetching web page…" meta=undefined
  channel_draft_texts=1
    [0] "📄 Web Fetch: Fetching web page…"

  --- fast path: https://example.com/ ---
  total=374ms
  start.meta="from https://example.com/"
  update_item_events=0
  channel_draft_texts=0

  === assertions ===
  PASS slow path produced update item events
  PASS slow path channel draft contains progress literal
  PASS slow path channel draft does NOT contain URL host
  PASS slow path update item meta is undefined (precedence fix)
  PASS slow path completion (real fetch returned)
  PASS slow path no duplicate emit
  PASS fast path produced no progress emit (no spam)
  PASS fast path no channel draft (no spam)
  PASS fast path completion
  PASS start meta carries URL (precondition for precedence fix)

  === verdict: PASS ===
  ```

- Observed result after fix:
  - **Slow path** (`httpbin.org/delay/3`): exactly **1** progress emit. The runtime emits a `kind:"tool"` update item with `progressText="Fetching web page…"` and `meta=undefined`. The channel draft formatter renders the visible text `"📄 Web Fetch: Fetching web page…"`. Fetch completes at `t≈4018 ms` with body.
  - **Fast path** (`example.com`): **0** progress emits, **0** channel drafts, fetch completes at `t≈374 ms`.
  - **Precondition demonstrated**: the start meta value at PR HEAD `94cdaad4` is `"from https://httpbin.org/delay/3"`. Without the precedence fix, that meta would render in the channel draft for the entire silent gap; the fix omits meta from the update event so the formatter falls through to `progressText`.
  - **Privacy**: the channel draft text contains the literal progress message plus the static `web_fetch` tool label (`"📄 Web Fetch:"`). No URL host, path, query, header, cookie, or body content.
- What was not tested: live delivery to a Telegram/Discord/Slack bot. `buildChannelProgressDraftLine` is the contract boundary every channel adapter consumes — it returns the exact text adapters send to their respective transports. The Tier-B harness exercises that boundary end-to-end.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: depends on httpbin.org availability and a typical `/delay/3` response within ~4 s; the harness asserts `slow.totalMs ≥ SLOW_DELAY_SEC*1000 - 500`. SSRF guard intentionally blocks loopback, so the slow URL is a real public-IP endpoint rather than a local server (the relevant property — TTFB > 1000 ms threshold — is reproduced either way).
- Before evidence: at the pre-fix branch HEAD (`33c013ed`), the channel draft for the same `httpbin.org/delay/3` invocation rendered `"📄 Web Fetch: from https://httpbin.org/delay/3"` for the full 3 s (the URL meta won the formatter precedence). After the fix at HEAD `94cdaad4`, the draft transitions to `"📄 Web Fetch: Fetching web page…"` ~1 s in.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run? — see the proof block above plus:

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/web-fetch.{cf-markdown,provider-fallback,ssrf,visibility}.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts \
                            src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental \
  --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
```

Result summary at current HEAD `94cdaad4` (all green): web-fetch.progress 10 / handlers.tools 92 (+2 new) / channel-streaming 22 / sibling web-fetch 81 / agent-runner-execution + followup-runner 190.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `src/agents/tools/web-fetch.progress.test.ts` (5 cases × 2 lanes, unchanged this round): slow path single emit; fast path zero emit; error cleanup; fail-open onUpdate; privacy.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts` (this round adds 2 cases):
  - existing `surfaces web_fetch partialResult` test extended to assert `meta` is undefined on the update event.
  - new `renders channel draft as 'Fetching web page…' (formatter integration with meta+progressText)` test: drives `handleToolExecutionStart` (which records the URL-bearing meta) plus `handleToolExecutionUpdate` (which now omits meta when progressText is populated), then passes the resulting item event through the real `buildChannelProgressDraftLine`. Asserts the rendered draft contains the literal and does not contain the URL.

What failed before this fix, if known? — no upstream test failure; ClawSweeper P1 flagged the formatter-precedence gap via source inspection.

If no test was added, why not? — N/A, formatter-integration test was added in this round.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) — **Yes.** Channel drafts now show `"Fetching web page…"` instead of `"from <url>"` during slow `web_fetch` calls (≥1 s). Tool result / error / cache / timeout / abort / retry / redirects unchanged.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) — **No.** No config keys, env vars, defaults, or migrations.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) — **No** for security / auth / secrets. The emit payload is a fixed literal; the channel-rendered draft is the literal + static `web_fetch` tool label only. SSRF guard, timeout, redirects, abort, retry, cache are unchanged. (The new user-visible progress line is captured under row 1.)

What is the highest-risk area? — The precedence fix omits `meta` from the update event whenever `nonExecProgressText` is populated. Any consumer that expects `meta` on EVERY item update (not just start) would see undefined here.

How is that risk mitigated? — In the current codebase the only path that reads `meta` from update items is the channel draft formatter, which is exactly what the fix targets. The start event still carries `meta` for consumers (UI activity-model, ACP, gateway) that snapshot at item start. The new formatter-integration test passes; the existing 22 `channel-streaming.test.ts` and 190 `agent-runner-execution.test.ts + followup-runner.test.ts` regressions still pass. The change is also gated by the `NON_EXEC_PROGRESS_SAFE_TOOLS` allow-list (currently `{web_fetch}`), so behavior for every other non-exec tool is bit-identical to pre-patch.

## Current review state

What is the next action? — Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? — Awaiting CI green and maintainer review on the remediation commit `94cdaad4`. No outstanding author work.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? — ClawSweeper round 1 (durable comment dated 2026-05-26T16:55Z): the P1 "Surface the web_fetch progress text in channel drafts" finding is fixed by `94cdaad4` (omit meta on update when progressText present); the Tier-B real-behavior proof above exercises the full tool → runtime → channel-draft chain and confirms the rendered draft contains the literal, not the URL meta.
